title: AI arbiter can be submitted when the selected chain has no arbiter address

evidence.quote: args: [form.beneficiary as `0x${string}`, resolvedArbiter, descriptions, amounts, 0, useAIArbiter, referrer],

reasoning: The render path explicitly handles a missing AI arbiter address, but the submit path still allows AI mode and passes the missing value into the contract args. That turns a known unavailable configuration into a wallet/contract failure after the pending state starts instead of blocking submission with a clear error.
recommendation: Before calling writeContractAsync, require a non-empty AI arbiter address when useAIArbiter is true. Disable the AI option or submit button for unsupported chains and show the existing not-deployed message as the actionable error.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests exercise AI arbiter selection on a chain where getArbiterAddress returns no address.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock getArbiterAddress to return undefined, select AI arbiter, submit the form, and assert writeContractAsync is not called and a clear error is shown.
minimumFixScope: Add a guard in handleSubmit and align the AI arbiter selector/submit disabled state with that guard.
